user_history_reviews.review_text:
Since there is no track listing at the time of this review I'll put one in:

1 - Heaven (I want you) [Club Mix] (6:58)
2 - Heaven (I want you) [Album Version] (5:15)
3 - Heaven (I want you) [Club Too Mix] (5:06)
4 - Who the hell is David Butler? (4:12)

This song came from Canouflage's third album, Meanwhile. It's probably their most controversial album in that the band took a detour from their normal electronic dance influences and experimented with more acoustic sounds. It turned out to be a great album creatively, if not one with lots of songs with dance-able electronic beats. This single has a couple of remixes for the song as well as B-side track from the Meanwhile album.

The Club Mix is essentially taking out the acoustic drums and guitar, then adding an understated breakbeat, created/programmed by Ron Aslan (from the EDM group Raw Stylus), and some additional synths to fill in the blanks. What you get is something a little more dancey and tailored to the club scene. The song is still light and peppy with no harsh beats or tones. To be honest the remix elements do little to make this track stand out. If it weren't for the core songwriting I would call it a dud. Personally I still prefer the original, but thanks to the source material it works.

The album version is a song that personifies the whole feel of Meanwhile. A bit laid back. A bit poppy. Acoustic drums and some guitars are peppered in with synthesizers in a light tune that has lyrics that belie a bit of bad blood in a lost love. It's actually a really good contemporary track. It's the kind of song that will get your head bobbing, even if it's not suited for the dance floor.

The Club Too Mix is very close to the Club Mix. It has the seam break beats done by Aslan and the same baseline, but the synth arrangements are a bit different. A bit more involved in the tune, if you ask me. I feel this version is a bit closer musically to the original album version, and deviates less from that structure. The Club Mix seems to want to move further away from the original while the Club Too Mix wants to keep closer ties. Of the two remixes I prefer this one more because of that.

Who the Hell is David Butler? is a very down tempo song with no singing or proper lyrics other than some radio broadcast type sound bites. It's a lusciously dark and moody track with a deep bass and low-fi style drums. This song is the last track on the Meanwhile album, and appears to be meant as a coda for the album. It's a wild departure from the happier sounding Heaven tracks, but I think the change in mood is a good showing of the contrast of textures you can find in the Meanwhile album.

The Heaven remixes aren't fantastic revisions of the original song, but then again it was the early 90's. Electronic music was changing back then and the club scene was still working out all of those different styles they wanted to identify with. If you ask me the Meanwhile album is a great buy for those who like a bit of creativity in their electronic artists, but if you are a tried and true club habitue then this single is a decent introduction.

Terminator and Terminator 2 had some theme music that was actually pretty nice. The original movie them had muted rhythms that kind of went off track while on Terminator 2 the theme music was grander and very much in sync with a strong industrial rhythm. Paffendorf took it upon themselves to put this melody into the dance floor with this single. Here is the track list:

Terminator II (Radio Edit)

Terminator II (Club Mix)

Terminator II (Old Skool Mix)

Airborn

Considering this was made in 1999 it's very trancey, or maybe progressive house for some of you. Very tight kick drum with the melody coming from a synth lead reminiscent of many songs from this genre. However add the counter-hooks, organ style bases and other outworld effect and you have a pretty addictive song on your hands.

The Club Mix starts a bit understated with said organs and whatnot and then about a minute in it builds into the anthem style theme that Terminator fans will recognize and rave fans drool over. I'm not much long mixes, but this Club Mix has enough variations in the song to keep it from getting boring. The Old Skool Mix is a tiny bit less in your face at the Club Mix, but it's no slouch. I would say it's a little more lower end with a deeper kick drum and the base line is a bit understated. Same anthem hook, but the tempo is slower so it feels groovier. Airborn is different song similar to the Club Mix with a strong trancey vibe to it.

Think of it as a peanut butter cup. If you like this style of music and like the Terminator music, then this is two great themes that sound great together.

This being an import I am assuming it's either going to be the German or the French 12". Either way the tracks are all identical to the U.S. release. There is nothing worth getting on this 12" vinyl you can't get on the original album unless you are a remix freak. Trust me. The remix (or dance version) isn't worth it. You get three tracks:

- Owner Of A Lonely Heart (Special "Red & Blue" Remix Dance Version)

This is considered a very rare track, but it's a mess. It's has a strong vibe on it similar to Dazz Band's Let it Whip along with various pacing breaks, sample clips (including a popular Art of Noise voice sample), and other distractions put in between normal spots of the song. It's an irritating club mix that runs a little over seven minutes.

- Owner Of A Lonely Heart (Edited Version)

Just a single mix of the album version. It's about a minute shorter than the album version and feels a bit stunted because of it. Although I admit it does hit all the important marks, but you miss some cool instrumentation.

- Our Song

This is the exact same version as what's found on the 90125 album.

If you are a major Yes fan and need all the mixes (including the bad ones) then I suppose you will find value in this 12". Everybody else just pass it up.

user_history_reviews.review_text:
Bought this watch in October as a gift for someone and it only lasted 3 months of wearing which caused some amount of embarrassment. The watch is decent and attractive, but, has the worse battery life and after a few updates being applied through Google Wear OS the phone refuses to display any time or information. Now the person is just stuck with a watch with a very big display and a blank screen.

Placing the watch on the charger produces nothing. The watch only provides a haptic feedback when holding down the middle crown button and refuses to budge or reset itself. Good luck to anyone who is considering purchasing this watch. I would recommend to take a look at the many issues being shared by user reviews, who bought this item, and were stuck with a dud in the end and go with your instincts and invest in another brand.

It's very sad to see a big company like Fossil and this particular defective item even being allowed to have it carried by Amazon.

DO NOT BUY !!!!

The right side of the stand (the side that has the additional USB ports) fits into the PS5 console. But the left side (and it says this in the instruction book) won't fit onto the console. It's supposed to only rest half way so it's not a snug fit. This stand was really made for the digital only version of the PS5. You also don't get any height difference off of the ground versus the stand that's supplied with the PS5 so it doesn't improve air flow imo. The white color of the stand does not match the PS5 console. It's also a bit of a clunky look. I ended up not to use this at all. It just doesn't look good. If I need more USB ports I'll just find an adapter that stays behind the console.

When the bag arrived I totally started cracking up. I just love the way it comes rolled up in a small round ball with the D20 printed on the mini bag container. It looks kind of like a great big 20 sided die in that compact state.
Opening the bag up to full capacity it's a great statement shopping bag that certainly lets anyone who is familiar with stranger things that you are a fan of the show and Dungeons and Dragons which these days who isn't.
The compact rolled up bag makes it easy to keep the bag clipped on a belt, stashed in a purse or in the case of a true 80's fan, clipped on to my rock & roll fanny pack. The clip on option is great for when you unexpectly need a shopping bag and hadn't thought to bring one with. It's so lightweight it barely adds anything except style to my fanny pack.

I typically wear a size XS and the extra small fits me great. It's snug but not too snug, just fitted. The ribbed texture really keeps everything in place along with the padding. I like that the top is a little longer than an workout bra but still has a cropped look that comes up above the belly. It's simple and sexy and a great look for the gym and very easy to move around and do aerobic exercise in.

It's also very long-lasting- to the point that later when taking it off, I used both an oil cleanser and makeup remover. Transferring is minimal. It actually stays a little sticky the whole time while wearing it, so that makes it a little surprising that it's so long lasting- since many liquids dry completely. It's not overly sticky, so I didn't find it bothersome.

Applies smoothly- it does feel like a velvety, whipped texture

If you're concerned about the appearance of lines on your lips with a liquid lipstick- I feel this is one makes them a bit pronounced.

Overall, for a liquid lipstick, I think it's pretty good. Has rich pigment and has a stand-out metallic look

Packaging is pretty

The skin on my neck has lost a lot of elasticity. I wouldn't say it's in bad shape but I feel it is for being in mid 30s. Having health stuff going on, former daily tanning machine user, former smoker and my weight goes up-and-down- all things that have contributed to my skin changes and my neck is no exception. This cream is soothing and leaves a lot of moisturize but not a lot of goop. I feel like my skin looks smoother, though I'm sure more consistent and long-term use will bring the most benefits and results. The texture of it is so creamy and thick and feels great on! Arrived sealed and packaged-up really well.

AE4FAMDNH6RVAJ27DI5M5I4YEUHQ.product_description: Mt. Hikami was once worshiped as a sacred place where a unique religion was taught. The water on this mountain is believed to house spirits, and there is a teaching from this region that “humans are born from water and return to it at the end of their lives.” As a result, “people looking for death” come to this mountain and experience their last moments while they come in contact with the mountain’s water. They believe this is the proper way to end their lives. Several peculiar rituals and customs still exist on this mountain, and many bizarre and unfortunate occurrences have supposedly taken place here. This story takes place on Mt. Hikami, a mountain believed to be a sacred place and revolves around Yuri Kozukata, Ren Hojo, and Miu Hinasaki who act as the three protagonists whose stories are interwoven in this mysterious and dream-like tale. Please note that English voice and subtitles are available via patch update in November. 30, 2021.

AEBJGDBVH3CT53MZG77WUWEVGGTA.product_description: Product Description The Dead Space 2 Collector's Edition celebrates the release of the new game by offering fans a highly prized bundle of limited edition items. Amazon.com Dead Space 2 is a third-person horror survival game in which players must battle an alien infestation straight out of a nightmare. Follow-up game to 2008's original Dead Space , in this new release players explore a whole new terrifying game environment and battle the Necromorph abominations utilizing new weapons and tools such as the powerful javelin gun and a new space suit with built-in booster rockets. Additional game features include: improved telekinesis powers and online multiplayer functionality. This special collector's edition for PC contains additional exclusive features (complete list below). Story Engineer Isaac Clarke returns for another bloodcurdling adventure in the sequel to the critically acclaimed Dead Space . After waking from a coma on a massive space city known as "The Sprawl," the lone survivor of a horrific alien infestation finds himself confronting a catastrophic new nightmare. Battling dementia, hunted by the government, and haunted by visions of his dead girlfriend, Isaac will do whatever it takes to save himself and dominate the gruesome onslaught. With an arsenal of tools to dismember Necromorphs and new determination, an engineer will bring the terror to space. The return of engineer Isaac Clarke in a whole new survival-horror adventure. View larger. Gameplay As with the original Dead Space game Dead Space 2 is a survival horror game played from the third-person perspective. Playing as Engineer Isaac Clarke players navigate a new environment, the space city, known as "The Sprawl." AEH27ZVAGEDIKGY6KHT43UPRINTQ.review_text:
I only had a little bit of experience with the original XCom PC game from the 90's. I remember not liking it, but I also remember not knowing the intricacies of the game nor the strategic benefits of a turn based system. Now that I am older and wiser I walk into this re-imaging of XCOM with excitement and anticipation. For the most part I wasn't disappointed. The game play is wonderful, the mechanics are challenging and the story makes me feel like I am taking part in updated episodes of the Gerry Anderson sci-fi classic U.F.O. on television.

XCOM is a turn based squad tactical game that also has elements of an RPG when you are outfitting and upgrading your troops and weapons. There is also a bit of SIM City as you build your headquarters. Lots of different gaming elements that really gel together if you ask me. Your squad is deployed in missions, which earn you money and other bonuses. The money is used to either upgrade your troops or expand and upgrade your base of operations (which in turn gives you various upgrades). As your troops successfully complete missions they gain experience that upgrades them to higher ranks (which gives you a chance to upgrade their skills). From time to time you get to capture alien corpses or technology for study (which then gives you yet another chance to upgrade to cooler weapons and defenses). As you can see the game gives a lot of options.

Your soldiers come in four different classes. Assault class for close attacks and quick deployment. Heavy class for big guns that do heavy damage. Sniper class for long range support. And finally the support class for maintaining the troops (but they can fight almost as effectively as assault class). Another class comes later in the game, but I won't give that one away. These soldiers can each carry two weapons (main and backup weapon) while the support soldiers can use grenades and med kits more effectively. Each class of soldier also has special abilities to make them more effective. All in all you have a simple yet engaging upgrade tree.

Your headquarters is another bit of strategy all together. It's an underground base with a command center, barracks, hangar and other rooms that you can fill with research labs, manufacturing facilities and more. You have a limited amount of space to fill in the facilities you need to be effective alien hunters. You have to plan out what you need as you won't be able to upgrade the base with everything available. It's fun adding rooms and planning out your base strategy, but it's limiting. I'll get to that later.

Combat, like the rest of the game, is turn based. I prefer this much better than real time strategy combat as you are able to get varied and personalized responses from each soldier instead of just huddling them together to attack a target. You can put your troops behind cover while other circle round, then you can come out and attack all at once putting your target in a crossfire. Or you can maneuver your snipers for better clearance while your assault troops draw your targets out of cover. You get a lot of different options with very little limits on how to take out your opponent. And you will need each one. These aliens aren't pushovers. While some missions may seem easy when you plan out a good strategy, they get much more aggressive and sneakier later on. You won't be able to use the same tactic over and over for long. You will need to learn to adapt if you want to maintain a high success rate.

Tactics and fighting is all well and good, but I am into a good story as much as fun gameplay. XCOM delivers there as well. The whole thing starts with your average alien invasion, but as the story goes deeper there is much more to these aliens and their agenda. I really love that about this game. By the time I finished it I felt like I was involved in a really cool sci-fi action movie. I won't give it away, but the ending it big.

While I have a lot to love about this game it's not perfect. Well yes the gameplay in combat, RPG and everything is pretty much perfect. The problem is the game feels limiting. It's awesome you get to upgrade and learn new tech, but in the end I felt like there would have been more to learn. More missions to play. More time to expand my headquarters. More opportunities to grow my team and fleet of interceptors. Basically the only real problem I have with the game is it didn't give me enough of all the awesomeness. If the game had more missions in between all that time along with more tech to learn that would make this game. Yes I know about the expansion, but I'm talking about the core game. And I believe the core game should have been longer and deeper into the story.

XCOM: Enemy Unknown is the perfect reboot of a beloved classic. Wargame fans, Sci-Fi gaming fans, TBS fans, even fans of 60's British Sci-Fi shows (yes... I'm talking to you U.F.O.) will absolutely love this game. I am hoping they make a new one for the PS4 with twice the content, because it's one of those games that you can't get enough of.

AF6ZXK5AR34RY2YOVKRLUGEGRCZQ.review_text:
I've bought this Assassin's Creed title to add it to my collection of "best so far" for this series. I became a fan of the Assassin's Creed series after playing Assassin's Creed 2 which upped the ante on so many levels within the gaming experience from the first title and further tweaked it with Brotherhood. The series captivates with the awesome story line moving from Altair to Ezio Auditore...and the series grew they (Ubisoft) branched out into other assassins expanding the story line...through Desmond Miles who had a link to the assassin's and their lineage through the various historic periods in time.

Brotherhood refined a lot of the game play as a single assassin earlier on in the game and as the missions unfold you are introduced to other characters in the game that lend support throughout. It's like having a team player co-op within a single player environment and each of the characters bring their own style of moves and assassination.

Apart from Assassin's Creed 2 & Brotherhood I was recently gifted Assassin's Creed 3 (collection growing) which I haven't played as yet since I like to go through the story lines and game play before moving on to the next title and hopefully I complete these it in time for the new title "Black Flag". Based on these story lines so far I am anticipating "Black Flag" to bring an all new feel to the series...I mean who wouldn't want to be an Assassin/Pirate who participates in high sea battles and plundering across the scenic islands of the Caribbean and the rest of the world...whilst taking on the Templars :)

AH5M56UWJNMMI3EBKUQPALNQG4ZQ.product_description: The newest chapters in the Pokémon series are coming to the Nintendo Switch system later this year. Catch, battle, and train Pokémon in the Paldea Region, a vast land filled with lakes, towering peaks, wastelands, small towns, and sprawling cities. There is no set path, so you can adventure freely through three grand stories. In one such story, you can challenge Pokémon Gyms in any order you desire as you aim for the Champion Rank! Explore a wide-open world at your own pace and traverse land, water, and air by riding on the form-shifting Legendary Pokémon Koraidon. Choose either Sprigatito, Fuecoco, or Quaxly, to be your first partner Pokémon before setting off on your journey through Paldea.  Pokémon in the Paldea region have the ability to Terastallize to gain special power. When a Pokémon Terastallizes, a Tera Jewel appears above its head like a crown, and the Pokémon’s body glistens like a cut gemstone. Each Pokémon has a Tera Type that remains inactive until the Pokémon Terastallizes. For example, most Eevee will have a Normal Tera Type, but some other Eevee have a Flying Tera Type! When a Terastallized Pokémon uses a move that matches its Tera Type and at least one of its original types, the boost to that move’s power will be even greater! Terastallizing holds the key to victory or defeat in battles in the Paldea region.  The Paldea Region is home to a prestigious school where people from all sorts of regions come to hone their skills against each other, be it through academics or Pokémon battles. The name of the school, its emblem, its uniforms, and other details will differ depending on whether you play Pokémon Scarlet or Pokémon Violet . Take classes with unique teachers who will show you what they know about battling, Pokémon biology, and more. Embark on an independent study called the Treasure Hunt to gain new experiences, meet new people, and find your very own treasure.
AH5M56UWJNMMI3EBKUQPALNQG4ZQ.review_text: Scarlet all the way! But do I choose grass, fire, or water? Very happy to have a new Pokemon adventure to play! Arceus was fun but Scarlet has an open world to explore--how exciting is that!? Arceus, Sports, Splatoon 3, Bayonetta, Scarlet & Violet....what a great 2022 for Nintendo fans.

Help them stop a robotic Emperor intent on conquering cross-dimensional worlds, with their own universe next in the firing line. Blast your way home with an arsenal of outrageous weaponry. Experience the Shuffle of dimensional Rifts and dynamic gameplay. Explore never-before-seen planets and alternate dimension versions of old favorites. (With compatible headphones. Dynamic 4K and HDR require a compatible 4K and HDR compatible TV or display.)
AH5M56UWJNMMI3EBKUQPALNQG4ZQ.review_text: Amazing graphics and fun characters make this my favorite PS5 game! The visuals in this world are incredible, it's a futuristic setting that at times I just want to take my time and enjoy the scenery. Holds up to any animated movie out there. Ratchet and Rivet are great characters. I'm hoping Rivet gets a game of her own next time. The storyline is simple and fun and the game play is entertaining. If you want a light-hearted game this is perfect. For comparison I also played Elden Ring recently and found it too dark for my tastes. Highly recommended!

[[VIDEOID:32df6c3003a43a69812c1b54feec721e]] ⭐ 11/8/21 Update ⭐

Below is my original review but I want to update that she's still working and she can also be used in the 2.0 update to invite her to The Roost cafe. She comes to the cafe alone - some NPCs bring along family/other characters. I don't have her new Amiibo card, or any other Isabelle amiibos, so I'm not sure if she brings any other characters with those Amiibos.

DLC/ Happy Home Paradise: When I scan her to try to design a paradise home for her it recognizes that's it's Isabelle but it says "You cant connect with this VIP client yet" so I'm assuming I just haven't unlocked that level yet to design a home for her. ⭐ HOWEVER, when I've completed designing other animals homes and ask to take pictures in their home- it allows for scanning some amiibos into the house to take pictures- it works to scan Isabelle for this feature. I have a Lottie Amiibo so I took a picture of them in Cally's home and posted it on this review to see. It's a lot of fun 😊🥳

If and when I get to the point of being able to design Isabelle's home- using this Amiibo figure- I'll come back to update again!

----------------------------------------
5/2021 Original review:

This is my first time using an Amiibo figurine. I've used the cards for some villager characters but not for special characters, like Isabelle.

I especially wanted the figurine to keep on display and figured if it happened to work on for Harv's on ACNH (Animal Crossing New Horizons) then it would be a bonus. It does work to use at Harv's. 🔴 Note that you can't invite the special characters to the campsite 🔴 We have both Nintendo Switch and the Switch Lite and this Amiibo works on both consoles.

Easy to use, just like with the cards. You place it near the joystick when the Amiibo symbol is on the screen and she pops right on the screen. 🔴 Also note that you can't change her clothes at Photopia/Harv's, you can only change her reactions and move her to different spots.

Once she appears there at Harv's, her poster is automatically ready to order on the Nook Shopping app- either from your Nook phone or at the ABD back at Resident Services. Like the other posters, it's 1,000 bells to buy it and it will arrive in your mailbox the next day.

The figurine is super cute and in great condition. The box was in great shape, as well!

AH6JLX5SJSUOJBBT6RRE2RDXIG6A.product_description: Animal Crossing Series 3-Pack Amiibo (Animal Crossing Series) - Tom Nook - Blathers Amiibo Bundle for Nintendo Switch - 3DS - Wii U (Bulk Packaging)
AH6JLX5SJSUOJBBT6RRE2RDXIG6A.review_text:
Reese, Cyrus and K.K. all came in great condition. Blathers and Tom came out of a box, which I knew before hand that they would, but they each have minor scuff marks so that's a little disappointing but I knew I was taking a bit of a risk with ordering these...and it's honestly not bad scuffing.

I wanted them to display as figures and to be able to scan at Harv's Island/Photopia on ACNH (Animal Crossing New Horizons). Each one scanned...nearly all scan quickly except for Reese, which first said there was no Amiibo found but every other time it has worked...though, I have to hold it longer than the others and in the same exact spot on the right joystick...the others aren't like that.

As long as Reese still at least scans, I'll keep my rating at 4-stars- if any further mess-ups happen, I'll update to make the rating lower.

Also, in case you were wondering, all of these characters, and the other special character Amiibos, are not able to be dressed-up/changed at Harv's. You can change their reactions and take pictures and then once they're scanned, it automatically becomes available to order the character's poster through Nook shopping. Also, K.K. doesn't have his guitar when scanned, so I personally feel he looks very different without it lol

All-in-all, other than the issues mentioned above, I love these. They're really cute and fun for AC fans 😍🙌🏼

AH4MUVG6B4Z65NTOXVH5CI6RJMVA.review_text:
If you look up fun in the dictionary, you'll see a picture of New Super Mario Bros 2. This game is honestly my favorite 3DS game, it ties with Super Mario 3D Land and Mario Kart 7.

Get this game! You will not regret it! Don't listen to all the people whining about how this game isn't creative: it doesn't need to be! This is the best New Super Mario Bros game out there, and it will provide you with countless hours of fun.

I never liked NSMBWii or DS too much, but I absolutely ADORE this game. This game is, without a doubt, a 3DS must have!

AH4MUVG6B4Z65NTOXVH5CI6RJMVA.review_text:
I just finished the entire game (all endings) and I don’t see any other reviews talk about this but there’s a lot of bugs in this game- even after I downloaded an update a few days ago. These include:

1) the game freezing when trying to load a cutscene, forcing you to restart. This happened more than once, and on one occasion I had to re-do an entire boss fight. There is nothing worse than having to redo like 30 min of gameplay due to something out of your control.

2) pop in everywhere! Entire buildings will just pop in. Graphically this looks like a PS3 game so I’m not sure why the draw distance is so poor.

3) wonky cameras during battle. Sometimes it felts like the camera had a mind of its own! No matter how many times i tried to keep it still, it would flail around. There were a few times i just wanted to rage quit because of how annoying the camera was.

4) a few times where the character model got stuck.

5) not a bug, but there are way too many invisible walls in this game. Places that look like they should be easily accessible will just straight up not let you go to them.

I give this game a 3 still because the story and music is absolutely incredible, but I can’t give it 5 because of how frustrating the bugs were.

AH4MUVG6B4Z65NTOXVH5CI6RJMVA.review_text:
As someone who is a fan of the GTA series, I went into this game thinking that it would a filler for my open world cravings until GTA V came out. But it turned out to be one of my favorite games of all time. I think it's worth noting that while this game and GTA games are similar in a lot of aspects (they both are open world games with stories involving criminals), they are also very different. GTA tries to remain somewhat serious, Saints Row the Third is just full on craziness.

The story of the game takes place after SR2 in a new town called "Steelport". You're gang is now very famous, but the government and various other gangs are trying to take you down. From the beginning of the game you are thrown in crazy situations, from robbing a bank to stealing a penthouse. And throughout the game's story you will explore a futuristic computer world as a toilet, fight off zombies, go to mars, and so much more. Aside from the story there are also countless activities to take part of, from Tank Mayham (where you try to cause as much damage with your tank in a limited time) to my personal favorite Professor Genki's Ethical Fun time (a TV show where you have to survive hordes of enemies and earn a certain amount of money to win). There are also a lot of collectibles scattered through out the city which are very fun to find. Finding collectibles and participating in activities help you take over the different parts of the city from other gangs.

This game has an immense amount of customization. You can create your character any way you want: male or female, black or white, fat or thin, strong or weak. You can even choose the voice acting from 7 different options (including a zombie voice). The character customization reminded me a lot of the Sims 3 character customization. Cars can also be customized to your liking. Most of the cars in the game can be taken to the car shop where you can choose the colors, tires, upgrades, etc. Cars save in your garage, so if you destroy a car you can just go back to the garage and retrieve it again. You can also customize your gang's style, the clothing you are wearing, the music that plays on the radio (the game has a wide variety of music) and the weapons you have. Lastly you can chose what your characters strengths and weaknesses are by earning "respect". "Respect" can be earned by completing activities, story missions and challenges. The "respect" can then be used to unlock upgrades, which you can then purchase. Upgrades include everything from health increases, to damage resistance, to more "homies" (or fellow gang members) fighting with you.

On of my favorite parts of this game was the ability to take screenshots whenever you wanted. The screenshots then get uploaded to saintsrow.com, where you can view them and save them to your computer or share them with others. I loved this feature; it was so fun to take pictures of my character doing random, crazy things.

There are some flaws with this game, however. Steelport is a rather bland town. Sure, it looks nice and the graphics are pretty good, but the majority of the town looks the same. It's easy to get lost because the houses look similar, the buildings look similar, and the stores look similar. The pedestrians don't really do much either, and you will see many of the same pedestrians over and over again. The games story is also a bit short, so don't expect to put a lot of time into this game unless you want to tackle all of the challenges and activities.

Overall, if you like fun and over the top open world games, then Saints Row the Third is for you. The most important thing for a game to be fun, and boy is this game fun!

AH4MUVG6B4Z65NTOXVH5CI6RJMVA.review_text: Before playing this, I played both Little Big Planet and Little Big Planet 2, so this game had some pretty high standards to live up to for me. Well, I can happily say that it not only met but exceeded my expectations. I was worried that because this was a hand held game, it would lose a lot of what was in the original game. But this one is practically as good as the ps3 games. I say practically because since it is a handheld, the levels are bit shorter and emptier than the PS3 games. But the game looks very good, the music is charming, and the customization from the original game is still there. If you loved Little Big Planet and Little Big Planet 2, then I highly recommend this game.

AHKTCN26AECQ5SZETO36C52OTS6A.product_description: Originally released in Japan in 1987 as the TurboGrafx-16 home console captivated gamers with its revolutionary graphics and sound, and still holds a special place in the hearts of fans today.
AHKTCN26AECQ5SZETO36C52OTS6A.review_text:
*Something I’ve discovered since posting this review, Castlevania X Rondo of Blood, is broken, the hearts do no replenish your health at all and this makes the game extremely difficult to beat when you can’t get to the bosses with enough energy left to consider facing them. Also, this is the biggest reason for buying the system, so without that the value takes a hit.

First, I think I would have like an American and Japanese version separate from each other.

I would have like Bonk and Bonk 3 both on the system and both in English. I would certainly have kept Castlevania X Rondo of Blood, as that was a great game, but I wouldn’t have brought many games that were in Japanese to English speaking countries, when you left off some great US Version Games.

If they kept Castlevania X and added a handful of other games, it would have been a lights out 6 out of 5 star system, but it isn’t, it’s only a 4 in its current form and I hope somebody finds a way to root it!

For the record I think these games should have been included.

Street Fighter II Duo
Bonk 1,2, & 3
Valis 2 & 3
Ys 3
All 3 Bomber Man Games
Loom
Dungeon Explorer II
Gates of Thunder
Sherlock Holmes (Nostalgia)
Dungeons and Dragons Order of the Griffin
Legendary Axe I & II
Beyond Shadowgate
Bloody Wolf
Cotton
Magical Chase

Take off all the Japanese games but Castlevania X and leave all the English Games, but add these to it and you’re gold!

So, it’s a good but, because there are titles that make the system worth the money, but it’s not the best effort that Konami could have put forth.

I would say 3 stars because of the missing games, but I gave it a 4, because it really is the best built and emulated system out of all the mini consoles.

AGLHRQLAWKWQKEF6VRO2Y3MRS4JQ.product_description: This world has gone through many Dusks, and is slowly nearing its end. Within this world, in the western reaches of the "Land of Dusk," there was a nation that prospered thanks to its use of alchemy. There, in order to survive the eventual arrival of the "Dusk End," the people devoted their efforts to rediscover and recreate lost alchemic technologies. Rediscovered technology from the past era was gathered in the alchemy research city known as "Central," where research was conducted on how to halt the advance of the twilight. One of the heroes is a young man who researched alchemy in Central, the other a girl living in a small town on the frontier. This girl's name is Escha. In the process of using her knowledge of ancient alchemy to help others, she was assigned to the Development Department. The young man's name is Logy. Having learned the newest alchemic techniques in Central, he requested a transfer to this understaffed town to make use of his abilities, and meets Escha when he is assigned to the Development Department as well. The two make a promise to use their alchemy together, and bring success to the Development Department.

AGLHRQLAWKWQKEF6VRO2Y3MRS4JQ.review_text: There isn't much to this game. It reminds me of some of the old homebrew games from the Commodore 64 era. It crashed a few times too for extra nostalgia. I'd give it a hard skip.

There aren’t many books available about Nalbinding and this is one of the best.

It covers a little of the history. I’ll disagree that we’r we can state categorically that nalbinding is older than knitting. We don’t know when knitting began. We don’t know when nalbinding began. While we know nalbinding is sturdier and can survive longer we don’t know that the oldest examples of both have survived and we don’t know that we’ve found the oldest surviving example of either. At best we can say the oldest of which we have a sample ID nalbinding. That’s at the core of what I didn’t like about this book. It faithfully parrots what has been said before without analysis.

What I love about the book is that it collates all of the common information into a clean, well presented volume. I call the information common, but it’s a slog to dig through it. Having a beautiful repository like this is gold.

It covers multiple ways to start (I agree with the author here that this is the hard part). It covers working on or off the thumb, how to work flat as well as in the round. It covers a few Finnish stitches, not many but the popular ones and enough to get started. It covers the Russian stitch as well and it covers the underlying principles behind these stitches. It’s worth it for that small section alone.

It finishes with a stellar collection of brilliant patterns. I love it so much. It’s getting a spot of honor on my crafting bookshelf.

AEMA3SW3WPNLEH3IACW23K2ZSUFA.review_text:
Not sure why they included Killer Instinct Gold but not the arcade or SNES KI titles.

I really like that they updated the controls for the N64 games, makes some things a tad easier in Perfect Dark. Sad to not see DK64 or Goldeneye in the line up, but totally understand why they are not here.

I was a huge fan of their work from the NES to the N64, never had an Xbox before, so I was unable to try out these other titles after they were bought by MS. Would have been fun to have seen a 100% complete collection worked out between the two giants, or a port of Rare Replay to the Switch. Rare Replay: Nintendo Edition... Probably omitting the 360 games due to compatibility issues, but including all those missing titles. IDK, sad that much of their work post Nintendo has not been nearly as great. Banjo Kazooie N&B is terrible, lol.

Amazon.com The layout of PlayStation2's Dual Shock 2 controller is nearly identical to that of the original PlayStation's Dual Shock controller, which is good news for most gamers.
